extra.reasoning: The property "globally(opted_out implies not sent)" is VIOLATED. The danger is transition t28: on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage >= 8), sent := true. This sets sent true while opted_out is true. opted_out is monotonic — only t1 sets it (to true), and nothing ever resets it to false. The stage forms a single unlock chain to the max value 8: stage 0->1 via key_1 (t3), 1->2 via key_0 (t6), 2->3 via key_0 (t9), 3->4 via key_2 (t12), 4->5 via key_0 (t15), 5->6 via key_2 (t18), 6->7 via key_0 (t21), 7->8 via key_2 (t24). Crucially, every stage-advancing transition only sets sent:=false and never touches opted_out, and each guard depends only on stage (not on opted_out), so opting out first does not block the climb. Counterexample (10 events, within the 11-event bound): 1) optout -> opted_out=true, sent=false, stage=0; 2) key_1 -> stage=1; 3) key_0 -> stage=2; 4) key_0 -> stage=3; 5) key_2 -> stage=4; 6) key_0 -> stage=5; 7) key_2 -> stage=6; 8) key_0 -> stage=7; 9) key_2 -> stage=8; 10) send_attempt -> stage=8 so stage>=8 holds and stage<8 fails, hence t28 (not t27) fires, setting sent:=true. Resulting state: opted_out=true AND sent=true, which falsifies (opted_out implies not sent).
